evals.prompt: Refactor OrderService.java to follow our Java 11 coding conventions. The class has mutable state, uses loops instead of streams, throws checked exceptions, uses null returns, and has methods doing too many things.
evals.expected_output: Refactored OrderService with: final fields, constructor injection, streams instead of loops, Optional instead of null, unchecked exceptions, small focused methods, all variables final (final var where obvious), immutable collections via List.of/copyOf or Collectors.toUnmodifiableList, no post-Java 11 features

assertions.text: All local variables and parameters use final (final var allowed)
No checked exceptions in method signatures
No for/while loops — all collection processing uses streams
Methods that may not find a result return Optional instead of null
All fields are private final
No method exceeds ~20 lines of logic
No post-Java 11 features used
Collections are immutable via List.of/copyOf or Collections.unmodifiable*

evals.prompt: Refactor UserProfile.java to follow our Java 11 conventions. It's a mutable JavaBean with getters/setters and imperative collection code.
evals.expected_output: Immutable UserProfile with: private final fields, constructor sets everything, no setters, immutable collections via List.copyOf, streams for collection methods, final var locals, no post-Java 11 features

assertions.text: All local variables and parameters use final (final var allowed)
No setter methods exist
All fields are private final
Single constructor that accepts and sets all fields
Collections are immutable via List.copyOf or similar
No for/while loops
No post-Java 11 features used

evals.prompt: Write a Java 11 service class called InventoryService that manages a product inventory. It should support: finding products by category, calculating total stock value, finding the most expensive product, and getting a summary of stock levels grouped by category. Use a ProductRepository dependency for data access.
evals.expected_output: New class following all conventions: final fields, constructor injection, streams for all collection ops, Optional for find methods, small focused methods, final var locals, List.of/copyOf where appropriate, no post-Java 11 features

assertions.text: All local variables and parameters use final (final var allowed)
All fields on InventoryService are private final
Dependencies injected via constructor
No for/while loops
Find methods return Optional
Each method does one thing
No post-Java 11 features used
